title: What to Expect in Markets This Week: May CPI Report, Updates from Tech Giants
description: May CPI Wednesday (est. 4.2% YoY), Apple WWDC Monday (Siri AI revamp, Tim Cook final event), SpaceX IPO Friday ($1.75T val, $75B raise). S&P 500 snapped 9-week win streak after blowout May NFP (172K vs 85K est). Nasdaq fell 4.7% on Friday. Bitcoin finished week ~$60K.
summary: What to Expect in Markets This Week: May CPI Report, Updates from Tech Giants — May CPI Wednesday (est. 4.2% YoY), Apple WWDC Monday (Siri AI revamp, Tim Cook final event), SpaceX IPO Friday ($1.75T val, $75B raise). S&P 500 snapped 9-week win str...

title: 2026 Midyear Investment Outlook: Constructive, Not Complacent
description: Morgan Stanley: S&P 500 year-end target 8,000, mid-2027 target 8,300 (12% gain). Overweight equities, underweight govt bonds. AI capex: top 5 tech firms spending $800B in 2026, $1.16T in 2027. Credit supply wave from AI financing. Dollar to stay weak in 2H 2026. Oil elevated through year-end.
summary: 2026 Midyear Investment Outlook: Constructive, Not Complacent — Morgan Stanley: S&P 500 year-end target 8,000, mid-2027 target 8,300 (12% gain)

title: Wall Street Week Ahead: CPI, Apple WWDC, SpaceX IPO, ORCL/ADBE Earnings
description: Core CPI est. 2.9% YoY. SpaceX IPO Friday (SPCX, $75B raise, ~$1.8T val). Apple WWDC Monday: AI strategy, Siri. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to testify on export controls/China. Earnings: ORCL, CHWY (Wed), ADBE, ACB (Thu), LEN (housing). World Cup kickoff Thursday.
summary: Wall Street Week Ahead: CPI, Apple WWDC, SpaceX IPO, ORCL/ADBE Earnings — Core CPI est. 2.9% YoY. SpaceX IPO Friday (SPCX, $75B raise, ~$1.8T val). Apple WWDC Monday: AI strategy, Siri. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to testify on export controls/China. ...

title: Global Airlines Slash 2026 Profit Forecast on Fuel Shock from Iran War
description: IATA nearly halved 2026 net profit forecast to ~$23B (from $41B). Jet fuel bill to hit $350B in 2026, nearly one-third of operating costs. Spirit Airlines already shut down. Middle East carriers likely to slip into red. Fares to stay elevated as capacity constrained.
summary: Global Airlines Slash 2026 Profit Forecast on Fuel Shock from Iran War — IATA nearly halved 2026 net profit forecast to ~$23B (from $41B)

title: Oil Crisis Hits World Growth Outlook as Forecasts Revised Down: Fitch Ratings
description: Fitch cut 2026 world growth forecast by 0.2pp to 2.4%. US -0.3pp to 1.9%, Eurozone -0.4pp to 0.9%. Brent crude 2026 assumption revised to $87/bbl (from $70). Strait of Hormuz assumed not reopening until July. AI/IT boom cushioning impact (US IT investment +18% YoY in Q1). Fed and BoE to hold rates in 2026, resume cuts in 2027.
summary: Oil Crisis Hits World Growth Outlook as Forecasts Revised Down: Fitch Ratings — Fitch cut 2026 world growth forecast by 0.2pp to 2.4%

title: Gulf Strikes and Fed Rate Hike Fears Keep Commodity Markets on Edge Ahead of CPI
description: US forces intercepted Iranian drones targeting Strait of Hormuz. Iran retaliated with ballistic missiles at Kuwait/Bahrain. Ceasefire collapsed. Gold below $4,320/oz (-4% wk), silver -8% to $67.5. WTI closed $90.5/bbl, Brent $93. May NFP 172K vs 85K est rekindled Fed rate hike fears. DXY surged above 100. Risk skew firmly to downside.
summary: Gulf Strikes and Fed Rate Hike Fears Keep Commodity Markets on Edge Ahead of CPI — US forces intercepted Iranian drones targeting Strait of Hormuz

title: Crude Oil Weekly Outlook: Will WTI Rebound from Key $88 Support Again?
description: WTI testing critical $88 support — lower boundary of multi-month consolidation. Bearish breakdown targets $74 then $67. Bullish breakout targets $127-$157 (Fib extensions). OPEC+ raising output 188K bpd for June. Supply shock enters 100th day. UAE Fujairah infrastructure expansion aims to bypass Hormuz.
summary: Crude Oil Weekly Outlook: Will WTI Rebound from Key $88 Support Again? — WTI testing critical $88 support — lower boundary of multi-month consolidation

title: Weekly EUR/USD Outlook: US CPI and ECB in Focus After Strong Jobs Data
description: EUR/USD plunged below 1.16. Support at 1.1500 → 1.1410 (March low). US NFP 172K vs 85K est, revisions +93K. Market now prices quarter-point Fed hike by year-end. CPI Wed (est. 4.2% YoY). ECB 25bp hike Thu widely expected. Lagarde forward guidance key. US-Iran deal speculation weighs on oil.
summary: Weekly EUR/USD Outlook: US CPI and ECB in Focus After Strong Jobs Data — EUR/USD plunged below 1.16. Support at 1.1500 → 1.1410 (March low). US NFP 172K vs 85K est, revisions +93K. Market now prices quarter-point Fed hike by year-end. CPI Wed (est...

title: Nvidia Expands AI Offerings with New N1X Processor Amid Market Decline
description: NVDA fell 6.19% to $205.10 as Nasdaq-100 dropped 4.7% (biggest drop since Oct 2025). Announced N1X processor and RTX Spark superchip for PCs, launching fall 2026. Targets Intel, AMD, Qualcomm, Apple. P/E 31.41x vs 5-yr median 30.39x. GF Score 95/100. Insider selling $388M past 3 months, zero buying.
summary: Nvidia Expands AI Offerings with New N1X Processor Amid Market Decline — NVDA fell 6.19% to $205.10 as Nasdaq-100 dropped 4.7% (biggest drop since Oct 2025)

title: Tesla Stock News: SpaceX IPO, TeraFab Chip Plant, JPMorgan Upgrade
description: TSLA closed $391 (-6.56% on June 5). SpaceX IPO priced at $135/share, $1.77T valuation. Elon in talks with ASML for $119B TeraFab chip plant (ASML CEO: Musk is "very serious"). JPMorgan upgraded TSLA to Neutral, PT $475. JP Morgan new analyst abandoned years of bearish calls. Robotaxi fleet shrank to 20 cars. Roadster demo pushed to August.
summary: Tesla Stock News: SpaceX IPO, TeraFab Chip Plant, JPMorgan Upgrade — TSLA closed $391 (-6.56% on June 5)

title: SpaceX IPO: Starlink Drives $1.8 Trillion Valuation
description: Starlink: ~10,000 satellites, >10M customers across 164 countries. Baron Capital: 15M subs by 2026, 300M by 2036. Projected 2036 revenue ~$500B, EBITDA ~$300B. Implied 2036 valuation ~$7.5T. Discounted PV matches current $1.8T IPO valuation. Requires Starship rocket for V3 satellites. Competing with Verizon, AT&T, Comcast.
summary: SpaceX IPO: Starlink Drives $1.8 Trillion Valuation — Starlink: ~10,000 satellites, >10M customers across 164 countries

title: WWDC 2026: Siri Biggest Upgrade Yet — Tim Cook Final Event
description: Keynote June 8. Siri becomes AI-powered chatbot with standalone app, multi-turn conversations, third-party AI integration (Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT). Apple signed multi-year deal for Google Gemini models. AI-generated wallpapers, Image Playground upgrades, camera app widgets. iOS 27 "natural language" Shortcuts. Tim Cook final major event before stepping down.
summary: WWDC 2026: Siri Biggest Upgrade Yet — Tim Cook Final Event — Keynote June 8. Siri becomes AI-powered chatbot with standalone app, multi-turn conversations, third-party AI integration (Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT). Apple signed multi-year deal for Goog...

title: Investors Pull Cash From Bitcoin ETFs as Crypto Slides — IBIT Loses $3.1B
description: BlackRock IBIT lost >$3.1B between May 18-June 3. Fidelity FBTC also leading exits. ETF redemptions force actual Bitcoin selling, creating negative feedback loop. BTC finished week ~$60K. ETFs supposed to smooth volatility instead amplify both rallies and selloffs. Easy brokerage access accelerates panic selling.
summary: Investors Pull Cash From Bitcoin ETFs as Crypto Slides — IBIT Loses $3.1B — BlackRock IBIT lost >$3.1B between May 18-June 3

title: Crypto Spot Volume Falls to $679B as Retail Demand Weakens
description: CEX spot volume fell to $679B in April — lowest since Oct 2023. Coinbase Q1 loss $394M, trading volume down 50% YoY. Bitcoin 45% below Oct 2025 cycle high. BTC at $61,822 with RSI-14 at 24 (deeply oversold). Google search interest for "cryptocurrency" down 70 points from Aug 2025 peak. $1.89B options expiry on June 5.
summary: Crypto Spot Volume Falls to $679B as Retail Demand Weakens — CEX spot volume fell to $679B in April — lowest since Oct 2023
